title: Install compressorjs and create file stubs
whatToBuild: Add compressorjs to package.json and install it. Create all empty stub files for the compressor feature: compressor.types.ts, compressor.store.ts, compressor.utils.ts, use-compressor.ts, compressor-settings.tsx, compressor-preview.tsx, compressor-image-panel.tsx, compressor-toolbar.tsx. Each stub exports only the named symbol (empty function or empty component) so downstream slices can import without errors. The existing image-compressor.tsx stub remains unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: compressorjs appears in package.json dependencies and is importable
All stub files exist and export their primary named symbol
TypeScript compiles without errors after installation

title: Compression pure utils + persisted Zustand store + unit tests
whatToBuild: Implement compressor.utils.ts with pure functions: a Promise-based compressImage wrapper around compressorjs, formatFileSize(bytes) returning a human-readable KB/MB string, computeCompressionRatio(originalBytes, compressedBytes) returning a formatted percentage string (e.g. '-68%'), buildExportFilename(originalName, mimeType) returning '{baseName}_compressed.{ext}', and isValidImageFile(file) type guard. Implement compressor.store.ts as a Zustand store with localStorage persistence holding all compressorjs parameters (quality, mimeType, maxWidth, maxHeight, minWidth, minHeight, width, height, resize, convertSize, checkOrientation) with sensible defaults and typed setters. Write unit tests for all pure utils functions and all store setters.
acceptanceCriteria: formatFileSize returns '1.00 KB' for 1024 bytes and '1.00 MB' for 1048576 bytes
computeCompressionRatio returns '-50%' when compressed is half of original
buildExportFilename('photo.png', 'image/webp') returns 'photo_compressed.webp'
isValidImageFile returns true for image/* MIME types and false otherwise
compressor.store.ts persists all settings to localStorage and rehydrates on reload
All unit tests pass with vitest

title: Debounced use-compressor hook
whatToBuild: Implement use-compressor.ts as a React hook that accepts a File (or null) and reads settings from compressor.store. When either the file or any setting changes, it waits 500 ms (debounce) then calls compressImage from compressor.utils. It exposes: compressedBlob (Blob | null), compressedObjectUrl (string | null), isCompressing (boolean), compressionError (Error | null), and a reset function. Object URLs are revoked on cleanup to prevent memory leaks.
acceptanceCriteria: Hook returns isCompressing: true while compression is running
Hook returns a valid compressedBlob after a file is provided
Changing a setting triggers re-compression after the debounce delay
Object URLs are revoked on unmount — no memory leaks
Hook returns compressionError when compressorjs throws

title: Image upload zone (drag-drop + clipboard paste)
whatToBuild: Implement a standalone upload component inside the compressor folder. It uses Ant Design Upload.Dragger for drag-and-drop and click-to-browse (accept='image/*', single file). It also registers a document-level paste event listener to capture clipboard images. On valid file selection it calls an onFileSelect(file: File) callback. On invalid file type it shows an error toast. The component is rendered in image-compressor.tsx when no image is loaded, and hidden once an image is selected.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging an image file onto the dropzone calls onFileSelect with the File
Clicking the dropzone opens the OS file picker filtered to images
Pasting an image from clipboard calls onFileSelect with the extracted File
Pasting a non-image from clipboard shows an error toast and does not call onFileSelect
Dropzone is hidden after an image is selected; a 'Change image' affordance replaces it

title: Compression settings form
whatToBuild: Implement compressor-settings.tsx as a form component that renders all compressorjs parameter controls bound to compressor.store: a quality slider (0–100, displayed as percentage), a mimeType select (JPEG / WebP / PNG), maxWidth and maxHeight number inputs, minWidth and minHeight number inputs, exact width and height number inputs, a resize strategy select (none / contain / cover), a convertSize number input (bytes), and a checkOrientation toggle switch. Each control reads from and writes to the store. The form is rendered above the preview area in image-compressor.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: All controls render and are interactive
Changing the quality slider updates the store's quality value
Changing mimeType select updates the store's mimeType value
All numeric inputs update their corresponding store fields
checkOrientation toggle updates the store's checkOrientation boolean
Settings survive a page reload (persisted via store)

title: Side-by-side preview panels with stats
whatToBuild: Implement compressor-image-panel.tsx as a reusable panel that renders one image (via src URL) and a stat strip below it showing: file size (formatted KB/MB), pixel dimensions (W x H), MIME type, and (on the compressed panel only) the compression ratio. Implement compressor-preview.tsx to compose two panels side by side — original on the left using the source File's data URL and metadata, compressed on the right using the hook's compressedObjectUrl and computed metadata. Show a loading skeleton on the compressed panel while isCompressing is true.
acceptanceCriteria: Original panel shows correct file size, dimensions, and MIME type
Compressed panel shows correct file size, dimensions, MIME type, and compression ratio
Compressed panel shows a loading indicator while compression is running
Both panels render images correctly after upload
Layout is responsive (stacks vertically on mobile)

title: Download toolbar
whatToBuild: Implement compressor-toolbar.tsx with a single Download button. When clicked it triggers a browser file-save of the compressedBlob using a filename derived from buildExportFilename (original file name + '_compressed' + extension from mimeType). The button is disabled when no compressed result is available or while compression is running.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Download saves a file named '{originalBaseName}_compressed.{ext}'
File extension matches the chosen mimeType (e.g. .webp for image/webp)
Button is disabled while isCompressing is true or compressedBlob is null
Downloaded file is a valid image that opens in an image viewer

title: Wire all components in image-compressor.tsx
whatToBuild: Replace the stub in image-compressor.tsx with the full orchestrated layout: ScreenHeader, upload zone (visible when no file selected), settings form above preview (visible when file is selected), side-by-side preview, and download toolbar. Wire use-compressor hook to provide compressed output to both the preview and toolbar. Handle error state by showing an Ant Design Alert when compressionError is set. Ensure the layout is responsive using the existing useResponsive hook pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Full upload → compress → preview → download flow works end-to-end
Changing any setting triggers re-compression and updates the preview
Error state shows an Alert with the error message
Layout is usable on mobile (single-column stack)
Navigating to /#/image-ocr/compressor renders the complete feature
No TypeScript errors in the compressor module
